vars.name: GATEWAY_PORT
vars.description: HTTP listen port for the WebSocket gateway.

vars.name: GATEWAY_READ_TIMEOUT
vars.description: HTTP server read timeout. Applies to the WebSocket upgrade handshake and HTTP request reads.

vars.name: GATEWAY_WRITE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: HTTP server write timeout.

vars.name: GATEWAY_IDLE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: HTTP keep-alive idle timeout. Connections idle longer than this are closed.

vars.name: GATEWAY_BACKEND_URL
vars.description: WebSocket URL of the ws-server backend the gateway proxies connections to. Must include the /ws path.

vars.name: GATEWAY_DIAL_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for dialing the ws-server backend when establishing a new proxied connection.

vars.name: GATEWAY_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for forwarding a single message between a client and the ws-server backend.

vars.name: GATEWAY_MAX_FRAME_SIZE
vars.description: Maximum WebSocket frame size in bytes. Frames larger than this are rejected before allocation. Default: 1MB.

vars.name: GATEWAY_PER_TENANT_CHANNEL_RULES
vars.description: When true, channel permissions are resolved per-tenant via gRPC streaming rules (Pro edition).

vars.name: GATEWAY_FALLBACK_PUBLIC_CHANNELS
vars.description: Channel patterns treated as public when a tenant has no per-tenant channel rules configured.

vars.name: REQUIRE_TENANT_ID
vars.description: When true, all WebSocket connections must identify a tenant via their JWT or API key. Disable only in single-tenant deployments where the tenant is implicit.

vars.name: DEFAULT_TENANT_CONNECTION_LIMIT
vars.description: Default maximum simultaneous WebSocket connections allowed per tenant when TENANT_CONNECTION_LIMIT_ENABLED is true.

vars.name: TENANT_CONNECTION_LIMIT_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, the gateway enforces per-tenant connection limits using DEFAULT_TENANT_CONNECTION_LIMIT as the default cap.

vars.name: GATEWAY_PUBLIC_PATTERNS
vars.description: Channel patterns accessible without authentication. Supports wildcards. Comma-separated.

vars.name: GATEWAY_USER_SCOPED_PATTERNS
vars.description: Channel patterns restricted to the authenticated principal. {principal} is substituted with the JWT subject at connection time. Comma-separated.

vars.name: GATEWAY_GROUP_SCOPED_PATTERNS
vars.description: Channel patterns restricted to a specific group. {group_id} is substituted with the JWT group claim at connection time. Comma-separated.

vars.name: GATEWAY_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, enforces per-authenticated-principal WebSocket message rate limits using GATEWAY_RATE_LIMIT_RATE and GATEWAY_RATE_LIMIT_BURST.

vars.name: GATEWAY_RATE_LIMIT_BURST
vars.description: Burst allowance for per-principal WebSocket message rate limiting. Allows short spikes above the sustained rate.

vars.name: GATEWAY_RATE_LIMIT_RATE
vars.description: Sustained message rate limit per authenticated principal, in messages per second.

vars.name: GATEWAY_PUBLISH_RATE_LIMIT
vars.description: Sustained rate limit for REST publish requests per authenticated principal, in messages per second.

vars.name: GATEWAY_PUBLISH_BURST
vars.description: Burst allowance for the REST publish rate limiter. Allows short spikes above GATEWAY_PUBLISH_RATE_LIMIT before throttling kicks in.

vars.name: GATEWAY_MAX_PUBLISH_SIZE
vars.description: Maximum REST publish payload size in bytes. Payloads exceeding this are rejected. Default: 64KB.

vars.name: GATEWAY_AUTH_REFRESH_RATE_INTERVAL
vars.description: Minimum interval between JWT refresh operations per connection. Prevents refresh storms under load.

vars.name: GATEWAY_AUTH_VALIDATION_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for validating auth refresh operations per connection.

vars.name: REVOCATION_MAX_LIFETIME
vars.description: Max time a revocation entry lives when exp is not provided

vars.name: GATEWAY_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum time the gateway waits for active connections to drain before forcing shutdown.

vars.name: WS_INTERNAL_SECRET
vars.description: Shared secret forwarded as X-Sukko-Internal-Secret to ws-server. Set WS_INTERNAL_SECRET_ENABLED=true on ws-server to enforce.

vars.name: SERVER_GRPC_ADDR
vars.description: ws-server gRPC address for RealtimeService

vars.name: PUSH_GRPC_ADDR
vars.description: Push service gRPC address for device registration and VAPID key endpoints.

vars.name: SSE_KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL
vars.description: SSE keepalive comment interval (prevents proxy timeouts)

vars.name: GATEWAY_CORS_ORIGINS
vars.description: CORS allowed origins (* = all, production: restrict)

vars.name: GATEWAY_CHANNEL_RULES_CACHE_TTL
vars.description: How long per-tenant channel rules are cached locally before re-fetching from the provisioning service.

vars.name: GATEWAY_REGISTRY_QUERY_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for querying the connections registry when enforcing per-tenant connection limits.

vars.name: WS_ADDR
vars.description: HTTP listen address for the WebSocket load balancer.

vars.name: WS_NUM_SHARDS
vars.description: Number of independent connection-routing shards. Higher values reduce lock contention; increase WS_BASE_PORT range accordingly.

vars.name: WS_BASE_PORT
vars.description: Base port for shard binding (3002, 3003, ...)

vars.name: WS_LB_ADDR
vars.description: Load balancer listen address

vars.name: SERVER_GRPC_PORT
vars.description: gRPC server port for RealtimeService (SSE Subscribe + REST Publish)

vars.name: KAFKA_CONSUMER_ENABLED
vars.description: When false, the Kafka consumer pool is disabled and no messages are consumed from Kafka topics. Useful for debug deployments or when using an alternative message backend.

vars.name: KAFKA_DEFAULT_PARTITIONS
vars.description: Default partition count for Kafka topics created on demand by ws-server.

vars.name: KAFKA_DEFAULT_REPLICATION_FACTOR
vars.description: Default replication factor for Kafka topics created on demand by ws-server.

vars.name: WS_MEMORY_LIMIT
vars.description: Memory limit in bytes. New connections are rejected (HTTP 503) when exceeded. Default: 512MB.

vars.name: WS_MAX_CONNECTIONS
vars.description: Maximum simultaneous WebSocket client connections per pod. New connections are rejected with HTTP 503 when this limit is reached.

vars.name: WS_MAX_KAFKA_RATE
vars.description: Maximum Kafka messages per second the server will consume across all topics.

vars.name: WS_MAX_BROADCAST_RATE
vars.description: Maximum Valkey broadcast fan-out rate in messages per second. Messages above this rate are dropped and counted in ws_broadcast_bus_dropped_total.

vars.name: WS_MAX_GOROUTINES
vars.description: Maximum number of goroutines allowed per pod. New connections are rejected when this limit is reached.

vars.name: WS_RATE_LIMIT_BURST_MULTIPLIER
vars.description: Burst capacity as a multiple of the steady-state rate

vars.name: WS_CLIENT_MSG_BURST_LIMIT
vars.description: Per-client inbound message burst capacity (token bucket). Clients exceeding this are disconnected.

vars.name: WS_CLIENT_MSG_RATE_PER_SEC
vars.description: Per-client sustained rate/sec

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, connection rate limiting is enforced per source IP and globally to protect against connection flood attacks. Controlled by CONN_RATE_LIMIT_IP_RATE, CONN_RATE_LIMIT_IP_BURST, CONN_RATE_LIMIT_GLOBAL_RATE, and CONN_RATE_LIMIT_GLOBAL_BURST.

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_IP_BURST
vars.description: Burst allowance for per-source-IP connection rate limiting.

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_IP_RATE
vars.description: Sustained new-connection rate limit per source IP address, in connections per second.

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_GLOBAL_BURST
vars.description: Burst allowance for the global (all-IP combined) new-connection rate limit.

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_GLOBAL_RATE
vars.description: Sustained new-connection rate limit across all source IPs combined, in connections per second.

vars.name: WS_CPU_REJECT_THRESHOLD
vars.description: CPU percentage above which new connections are rejected with HTTP 503. Hysteresis: stop rejecting below WS_CPU_REJECT_THRESHOLD_LOWER.

vars.name: WS_CPU_REJECT_THRESHOLD_LOWER
vars.description: Lower: stop rejecting below this % (0 = auto: upper - 10)

vars.name: WS_CPU_PAUSE_THRESHOLD
vars.description: CPU percentage above which Kafka consumption is paused. Hysteresis: resume below WS_CPU_PAUSE_THRESHOLD_LOWER.

vars.name: WS_CPU_PAUSE_THRESHOLD_LOWER
vars.description: Lower: resume Kafka below this % (0 = auto: upper - 10)

vars.name: WS_CPU_EWMA_BETA
vars.description: EWMA decay factor for CPU smoothing (0-1, higher = smoother)

vars.name: TCP_LISTEN_BACKLOG
vars.description: TCP accept queue size (0 = Go default ~128)

vars.name: METRICS_INTERVAL
vars.description: Prometheus metrics collection interval for system-level metrics (CPU usage, memory, connection counts).

vars.name: CPU_POLL_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for CPU usage sampling used by backpressure and connection rejection decisions. Lower values detect spikes faster at marginally higher overhead.

vars.name: ALERT_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, ws-server sends operational alerts to the configured destinations (ALERT_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, ALERT_CONSOLE_ENABLED).

vars.name: ALERT_MIN_LEVEL
vars.description: Minimum alert severity to send. One of: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL.

vars.name: ALERT_SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL
vars.description: Slack incoming webhook URL for alert delivery.

vars.name: ALERT_SLACK_CHANNEL
vars.description: Slack channel to post alerts to (e.g. #ops-alerts).

vars.name: ALERT_SLACK_USERNAME
vars.description: Display name for the Slack alert bot.

vars.name: ALERT_SLACK_TIMEOUT
vars.description: HTTP request timeout for each Slack webhook delivery attempt.

vars.name: ALERT_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW
vars.description: Window duration for alert rate limiting. At most ALERT_RATE_LIMIT_MAX alerts are sent per window.

vars.name: ALERT_RATE_LIMIT_MAX
vars.description: Maximum alerts sent per ALERT_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW. Excess alerts are suppressed to prevent notification floods.

vars.name: ALERT_CONSOLE_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, alerts are written to the structured log output in addition to any remote destinations.

vars.name: VALKEY_ADDRS
vars.description: Valkey server addresses, comma-separated. For Sentinel high-availability, provide all Sentinel addresses (e.g. sentinel1:26379,sentinel2:26379,sentinel3:26379). For single-instance, provide one address.

vars.name: VALKEY_MASTER_NAME
vars.description: Valkey Sentinel master name. Only used when VALKEY_ADDRS contains Sentinel addresses.

vars.name: VALKEY_PASSWORD
vars.description: Valkey authentication password.

vars.name: VALKEY_DB
vars.description: Valkey database index. Use 0 (the default) unless your deployment requires database isolation.

vars.name: VALKEY_CHANNEL
vars.description: Valkey pub/sub channel prefix. The full per-tenant channel is {VALKEY_CHANNEL}:{tenantID} (e.g. ws.broadcast:acme-corp). All ws-server pods must use the same prefix. If Valkey ACLs restrict pub/sub by channel pattern, update patterns from ws.broadcast to ws.broadcast:*.

vars.name: WS_SLOW_CLIENT_MAX_ATTEMPTS
vars.description: Number of consecutive failed send attempts before a slow client is disconnected. A send attempt fails when the client's outbound buffer is full.

vars.name: WS_CLIENT_SEND_BUFFER_SIZE
vars.description: Per-client outbound message buffer size in messages. Larger buffers tolerate temporary client slowness at the cost of higher memory usage per connection.

vars.name: WS_GAP_NOTIFY_BUFFER_SIZE
vars.description: Per-client buffer for gap notification messages. Gap notifications are low-priority; this buffer absorbs bursts without blocking the main message pipeline.

vars.name: WS_REPLAY_RATE_LIMIT_INTERVAL
vars.description: Minimum interval between history replay requests per client per channel. Prevents replay storms from a single connection.

vars.name: BROADCAST_TYPE
vars.description: Backend for inter-instance message broadcasting. Only valkey is supported — Valkey pub/sub is required for all ws-server deployments.

vars.name: VALKEY_TLS_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, Valkey connections use TLS encryption. Required for managed Valkey/Redis services (ElastiCache, Memorystore, Upstash).

vars.name: VALKEY_TLS_INSECURE
vars.description: When true, TLS certificate verification is skipped for Valkey connections. Do not use in production.

vars.name: VALKEY_TLS_CA_PATH
vars.description: Path to a custom CA certificate file for Valkey TLS connections. Required when Valkey uses a private certificate authority.

vars.name: TOPIC_REFRESH_INTERVAL
vars.description: How often ws-server re-syncs the tenant topic list from the provisioning registry. This is a safety net; the primary update path is event-driven.

vars.name: WS_PONG_WAIT
vars.description: Timeout for receiving a pong response after sending a ping. Clients that do not respond within this window are disconnected. Must be greater than WS_PING_PERIOD.

vars.name: WS_PING_PERIOD
vars.description: Interval between WebSocket ping frames sent to connected clients. Must be less than WS_PONG_WAIT.

vars.name: WS_WRITE_WAIT
vars.description: Timeout for a single WebSocket write operation. Increase for high-latency networks.

vars.name: WS_REPLAY_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for a single history replay handler to complete.

vars.name: WS_PUBLISH_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for a client publish operation (client to gateway to ws-server to Kafka).

vars.name: WS_MAX_REPLAY_MESSAGES
vars.description: Maximum number of messages returned per history replay request.

vars.name: WS_TOPIC_CREATION_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for on-demand Kafka topic creation.

vars.name: WS_SHARD_DIAL_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for the load balancer to dial a shard when proxying a new connection.

vars.name: WS_SHARD_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for forwarding a single message between the load balancer and a shard.

vars.name: WS_METRICS_AGGREGATION_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for aggregating per-shard metrics into cluster-level summaries.

vars.name: WS_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_PERIOD
vars.description: Maximum time ws-server waits for in-flight connections and consumers to drain during graceful shutdown.

vars.name: WS_SHUTDOWN_CHECK_INTERVAL
vars.description: Polling interval for checking whether all connections have closed during graceful shutdown.

vars.name: WS_METRICS_COLLECT_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for collecting internal runtime metrics (goroutine counts, buffer saturation).

vars.name: WS_MEMORY_MONITOR_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for polling memory usage against WS_MEMORY_LIMIT.

vars.name: WS_MEMORY_WARNING_PERCENT
vars.description: Memory usage percentage of WS_MEMORY_LIMIT at which a warning log is emitted.

vars.name: WS_MEMORY_CRITICAL_PERCENT
vars.description: Memory usage percentage of WS_MEMORY_LIMIT at which new connections are rejected.

vars.name: WS_BUFFER_SAMPLE_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for sampling client send buffer saturation metrics.

vars.name: WS_BUFFER_MAX_SAMPLES
vars.description: Rolling window size in samples for buffer saturation statistics.

vars.name: WS_BUFFER_HIGH_SATURATION_PERCENT
vars.description: Per-client send buffer fill percentage above which the client is considered saturated.

vars.name: WS_BUFFER_POPULATION_WARN_PERCENT
vars.description: Percentage of clients considered saturated above which a warning alert is triggered.

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_IP_TTL
vars.description: How long per-IP connection rate limit state is retained after the last connection attempt from that IP.

vars.name: CONN_RATE_LIMIT_CLEANUP_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for purging expired per-IP rate limit state.

vars.name: BROADCAST_BUFFER_SIZE
vars.description: Per-tenant per-shard broadcast message buffer size. When this buffer is full, messages for that tenant are dropped and counted in ws_broadcast_bus_dropped_total. Maximum: 65,536 (BROADCAST_BUFFER_SIZE_MAX); values above this cause a startup failure. Operators running high-throughput single-tenant workloads should increase this value.

vars.name: BROADCAST_SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for draining the broadcast bus during graceful shutdown.

vars.name: VALKEY_WRITE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for Valkey write operations (SET, EXPIRE) from the broadcast bus.

vars.name: VALKEY_PUBLISH_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for each Valkey PUBLISH command in the broadcast hot path.

vars.name: VALKEY_STARTUP_PING_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for the Valkey connectivity check during ws-server startup.

vars.name: VALKEY_RECONNECT_INITIAL_BACKOFF
vars.description: Starting backoff delay for Valkey reconnection attempts after a connection failure.

vars.name: VALKEY_RECONNECT_MAX_BACKOFF
vars.description: Maximum backoff delay between Valkey reconnection attempts.

vars.name: VALKEY_RECONNECT_MAX_ATTEMPTS
vars.description: Maximum number of Valkey reconnection attempts before the broadcast bus is marked unhealthy.

vars.name: VALKEY_HEALTH_CHECK_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for periodic Valkey health checks.

vars.name: VALKEY_HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for each Valkey health check PING.

vars.name: VALKEY_PUBLISH_STALENESS_THRESHOLD
vars.description: Log warning if no publish within this window

vars.name: KAFKA_BATCH_SIZE
vars.description: Maximum number of Kafka messages processed per consumer batch.

vars.name: KAFKA_BATCH_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum time to wait to fill a batch before processing it.

vars.name: KAFKA_FETCH_MAX_WAIT
vars.description: Maximum time the Kafka broker waits before returning a fetch response, even if KAFKA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES has not been met. Lower values reduce latency; higher values improve throughput.

vars.name: KAFKA_FETCH_MIN_BYTES
vars.description: Minimum bytes a broker should accumulate before returning a fetch response.

vars.name: KAFKA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES
vars.description: Maximum bytes returned per fetch request.

vars.name: KAFKA_SESSION_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Kafka consumer group session timeout. If the consumer does not heartbeat within this window, it is declared dead and a rebalance is triggered. Must be within the broker's configured range.

vars.name: KAFKA_REBALANCE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum time allowed for a consumer group rebalance to complete. Must be greater than KAFKA_COMMIT_ON_REVOKE_TIMEOUT.

vars.name: KAFKA_REPLAY_FETCH_MAX_BYTES
vars.description: Maximum bytes per fetch during history replay. Kept separate from KAFKA_FETCH_MAX_BYTES to prevent replay reads from saturating the live consumer path.

vars.name: KAFKA_BACKPRESSURE_CHECK_INTERVAL
vars.description: Polling interval for the Kafka backpressure circuit breaker (CPU and goroutine pressure check).

vars.name: KAFKA_COMMIT_ON_REVOKE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: How long ws-server waits to commit processed Kafka offsets during a consumer group rebalance (triggered on rolling deploys, pod restarts, or scale events). If the commit completes within this window, duplicate delivery to clients by the new consumer is minimized. If the commit times out, offsets since the last background auto-commit (see KAFKA_AUTO_COMMIT_INTERVAL) may be re-delivered.

vars.name: KAFKA_AUTO_COMMIT_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval between background Kafka offset commits. Controls the crash-path duplicate window: if a pod crashes without a clean shutdown, messages processed in the final KAFKA_AUTO_COMMIT_INTERVAL before the crash may be re-delivered after failover. Lower values reduce the crash-path window at the cost of more frequent broker commits. Minimum: 100ms — values below this cause a startup validation error.

vars.name: KAFKA_PRODUCER_BATCH_MAX_BYTES
vars.description: Maximum bytes per Kafka producer batch.

vars.name: KAFKA_PRODUCER_MAX_BUFFERED_RECORDS
vars.description: Maximum number of records buffered in the Kafka producer before backpressure is applied to callers.

vars.name: KAFKA_PRODUCER_RECORD_RETRIES
vars.description: Number of retries for failed Kafka produce operations before the record is discarded.

vars.name: KAFKA_PRODUCER_CB_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Circuit breaker observation window for Kafka producer failures.

vars.name: KAFKA_PRODUCER_CB_MAX_FAILURES
vars.description: Maximum producer failures within the circuit breaker window before the producer circuit opens (pauses).

vars.name: KAFKA_PRODUCER_CB_HALF_OPEN_REQS
vars.description: Number of test requests permitted when the Kafka producer circuit breaker is in half-open state.

vars.name: WS_ROUTING_FANOUT_WORKERS
vars.description: Number of parallel workers for routing message fan-out to subscribed clients. Increase for high-subscription-count workloads.

vars.name: WS_ROUTING_FANOUT_QUEUE_SIZE
vars.description: Internal queue depth for the routing fan-out worker pool.

vars.name: WS_ROUTING_DLQ_MAX_RETRIES
vars.description: Maximum retry attempts for a failed message delivery before it is discarded.

vars.name: WS_ROUTING_DLQ_BASE_DELAY
vars.description: Initial retry delay for failed message deliveries (exponential backoff starting point).

vars.name: WS_ROUTING_DLQ_MAX_DELAY
vars.description: Maximum retry delay cap for failed message deliveries.

vars.name: WS_ROUTING_DLQ_RETRY_WORKERS
vars.description: Number of parallel workers processing the dead-letter retry queue.

vars.name: WS_MAX_CHANNELS_PER_CLIENT
vars.description: Maximum concurrent channel subscriptions per client connection. Subscription requests beyond this limit are rejected.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, message history is retained in a Valkey Streams ring buffer with Kafka as a fallback for deeper replay. Enables client history replay requests.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_BUFFER_DEPTH
vars.description: Maximum number of messages retained per channel in the Valkey Streams ring buffer (XADD MAXLEN ~). Older messages are evicted when the limit is reached.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_TTL
vars.description: Per-stream Valkey key expiry. Streams inactive longer than this duration are deleted to free memory.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_WRITER_BUFFER
vars.description: Internal message queue depth for the history writer. Messages are dropped when this buffer is full.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_MAX_LIMIT
vars.description: Maximum number of messages a client may request in a single history replay call.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_DELIVERY_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Deadline for completing a single history replay request, including Valkey XREVRANGE and optional Kafka fallback reads.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_WRITER_PIPELINE_BATCH
vars.description: Maximum number of Valkey XADD commands per pipeline batch for history writes.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_WRITER_LOCK_TTL
vars.description: TTL for the distributed history writer lock in Valkey. The active writer pod renews this lock on each heartbeat interval.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_WRITER_RESTART_INITIAL_BACKOFF
vars.description: Initial delay before restarting the history writer after a failure.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_WRITER_RESTART_MAX_BACKOFF
vars.description: Maximum delay between history writer restart attempts.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_VALKEY_CMD_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Per-command timeout for history Valkey operations (XADD, XREVRANGE, EXPIRE).

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_LOCK_FAILURES
vars.description: Number of consecutive heartbeat lock renewal failures before the history writer steps down and triggers a restart.

vars.name: WS_HISTORY_WRITER_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for history writer lock renewal and broadcast bus health checks.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, ws-server tracks active connections in Valkey and exposes them via the Connections Management API (Pro edition). Requires Valkey memory headroom proportional to active connections x subscriptions x average channel name length.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_TTL
vars.description: Valkey EXPIRE duration for each connection entry in the registry. Heartbeats renew the TTL; connections that fail to heartbeat within this window are treated as expired.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_BUFFER
vars.description: Internal channel buffer for registry events (connect, disconnect, subscribe). Events are dropped when this buffer is full.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_FLUSH_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for flushing buffered registry events to Valkey.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for renewing active connection TTLs in the registry.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_RESTART_INITIAL_BACKOFF
vars.description: Initial delay before restarting the registry writer after a failure.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_RESTART_MAX_BACKOFF
vars.description: Maximum delay between registry writer restart attempts.

vars.name: WS_CONNECTIONS_REGISTRY_SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Time allowed to flush buffered registry events during graceful shutdown. Set to 0 to skip draining.

vars.name: WS_ADMIN_CHANNEL_SUBSCRIBE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for each shard's SUBSCRIBE call to the admin broadcast channel at startup.

vars.name: WS_INTERNAL_SECRET_ENABLED
vars.description: When true, ws-server validates the X-Sukko-Internal-Secret header on all incoming connections, rejecting any that bypass the gateway.

vars.name: WS_INTERNAL_SECRET
vars.description: Shared secret validated as X-Sukko-Internal-Secret on incoming connections. Must match the value configured on the gateway. Required when WS_INTERNAL_SECRET_ENABLED is true.

vars.name: ANALYTICS_PARTMAN_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for running pg_partman maintenance on analytics partition tables.

vars.name: ANALYTICS_SSE_MAX_CONNS
vars.description: Maximum concurrent SSE connections for the analytics streaming endpoint.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_KAFKA_BROKERS
vars.description: Kafka broker addresses for provisioning-originated admin operations (topic creation, etc.). Comma-separated.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_ADDR
vars.description: HTTP listen address for the provisioning API.

vars.name: GRPC_PORT
vars.description: gRPC server port for internal service-to-service communication.

vars.name: ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_KEY
vars.description: Bootstrap admin API key for provisioning service. Required; used for initial tenant setup.

vars.name: DEFAULT_PARTITIONS
vars.description: Default Kafka partition count assigned to new tenant topics.

vars.name: DEFAULT_RETENTION_MS
vars.description: Default Kafka topic retention in milliseconds. Default: 604,800,000ms (7 days).

vars.name: MAX_TOPICS_PER_TENANT
vars.description: Default maximum number of Kafka topics per tenant.

vars.name: MAX_PARTITIONS_PER_TENANT
vars.description: Default maximum total partitions across all topics per tenant.

vars.name: MAX_STORAGE_BYTES
vars.description: Default maximum Kafka storage quota per tenant in bytes. Default: 10,737,418,240 (10GB).

vars.name: PRODUCER_BYTE_RATE
vars.description: Default Kafka producer byte-rate quota per tenant in bytes/sec. Default: 10485760 (10MB/s).

vars.name: CONSUMER_BYTE_RATE
vars.description: Default Kafka consumer byte-rate quota per tenant in bytes/sec. Default: 52428800 (50MB/s).

vars.name: DEPROVISION_GRACE_DAYS
vars.description: Days to retain a deprovisioned tenant's Kafka topics and ACLs before permanent deletion.

vars.name: LIFECYCLE_CHECK_INTERVAL
vars.description: How often the lifecycle manager scans for tenants due for deprovisioning or cleanup.

vars.name: LIFECYCLE_MANAGER_ENABLED
vars.description: Enables the background tenant lifecycle manager (deprovisioning, retention enforcement).

vars.name: MAX_ROUTING_RULES_PER_TENANT
vars.description: Max routing rules per tenant

vars.name: MAX_TOPICS_PER_RULE
vars.description: Max topics per routing rule

vars.name: ROUTING_DLQ_TOPIC_PARTITIONS
vars.description: Partitions for dead-letter topics

vars.name: ROUTING_DLQ_TOPIC_RETENTION_MS
vars.description: Retention for dead-letter topics (7 days)

vars.name: INFRA_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR
vars.description: Replication factor for all infrastructure topics (DLQ + default); use int — caarlos0/env v11 does not handle int16; cast to int16 at CreateTopic call sites

vars.name: API_RATE_LIMIT_PER_MIN
vars.description: Maximum provisioning API requests per minute per IP before HTTP 429 is returned.

vars.name: KEY_REGISTRY_REFRESH_INTERVAL
vars.description: How often the key registry polls for updated tenant JWT signing keys.

vars.name: KEY_REGISTRY_QUERY_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for each key registry lookup query.

vars.name: SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum time to wait for in-flight requests during graceful shutdown.

vars.name: CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS
vars.description: Allowed origins for CORS preflight responses. Comma-separated; no wildcard in production.

vars.name: CORS_MAX_AGE
vars.description: Maximum seconds a CORS preflight response may be cached by the browser.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_MAX_TENANTS_FETCH_LIMIT
vars.description: Maximum number of tenants returned by internal bulk-fetch operations.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_DELETION_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for a single tenant deletion operation (Kafka topic removal, ACL cleanup).

vars.name: SLUG_RENAME_TOPIC_HOLD_PERIOD
vars.description: Retention window for old Kafka topics and ACLs after a tenant slug rename. Minimum 24h.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_TOKEN_REVOCATION_MAX_LIFETIME
vars.description: Maximum duration to retain a revoked token record before the prune loop removes it.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_BULK_DISCONNECT_CONCURRENCY
vars.description: Maximum concurrent Valkey publishes during a bulk connection disconnect request.

vars.name: MAX_WEBHOOKS_PER_TENANT
vars.description: Maximum number of webhook endpoint registrations per tenant.

vars.name: WEBHOOK_DOWNGRADE_POLL_INTERVAL
vars.description: Polling interval for checking whether a tenant's webhook delivery should pause due to edition downgrade.

vars.name: WEBHOOK_WORKER_GRPC_PORT
vars.description: gRPC listen port for the webhook-worker internal service. Must differ from GRPC_PORT.

vars.name: WEBHOOK_WORKER_GRPC_ADDR
vars.description: gRPC address provisioning dials to reach the webhook-worker service. Required for Pro/Enterprise editions.

vars.name: LOG_LEVEL
vars.description: Logging level (debug, info, warn, error). info is recommended for production.

vars.name: LOG_FORMAT
vars.description: Log output format: json for machine parsing (production), text for human-readable (development).

vars.name: ENVIRONMENT
vars.description: Deployment environment name (e.g. local, dev, stag, prod). Used for Kafka topic namespace resolution.

vars.name: SUKKO_LICENSE_KEY
vars.description: Sukko license token (JWT). Community edition has no key; Pro/Enterprise obtain via Sukko account. Leave empty for Community.

vars.name: OTEL_TRACING_ENABLED
vars.description: Enable distributed tracing via OpenTelemetry. Disabled by default; configure OTEL_EXPORTER_TYPE and OTEL_EXPORTER_ENDPOINT when enabled.

vars.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_TYPE
vars.description: OpenTelemetry trace exporter type (otlp, stdout, or empty to disable tracing).

vars.name: OTEL_EXPORTER_ENDPOINT
vars.description: OpenTelemetry collector gRPC endpoint (e.g. otelcol:4317). Required when OTEL_EXPORTER_TYPE=otlp.

vars.name: PPROF_ENABLED
vars.description: Enable Go pprof profiling endpoints (/debug/pprof/). Disabled by default; enable only in controlled environments.

vars.name: PYROSCOPE_ENABLED
vars.description: Enable continuous profiling via Pyroscope. Disabled by default.

vars.name: PYROSCOPE_ADDR
vars.description: Pyroscope server address (e.g. http://pyroscope:4040). Required when PYROSCOPE_ENABLED=true.

vars.name: AUTH_MODE
vars.description: Authentication enforcement mode. Only "required" is supported; tokens without valid signatures are rejected.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_GRPC_ADDR
vars.description: gRPC address of the provisioning service for internal communication (e.g. channel rule streaming).

vars.name: HTTP_READ_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum duration for reading a complete HTTP request. Protects against slow-loris attacks.

vars.name: HTTP_WRITE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum duration for writing the HTTP response.

vars.name: HTTP_IDLE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Maximum idle time on a keep-alive connection before it is closed.

vars.name: KAFKA_TOPIC_NAMESPACE_OVERRIDE
vars.description: Override for the Kafka topic namespace (normally derived from ENVIRONMENT). Forbidden in production — causes startup failure when ENVIRONMENT=prod.

vars.name: VALID_NAMESPACES
vars.description: Comma-separated list of allowed Kafka topic namespaces. Used to validate KAFKA_TOPIC_NAMESPACE_OVERRIDE.

vars.name: MESSAGE_BACKEND
vars.description: Message ingestion backend: direct (no persistence, no Kafka dependency) or kafka (full Kafka/Redpanda integration with replay).

vars.name: KAFKA_BROKERS
vars.description: Comma-separated Kafka/Redpanda broker addresses. Required when MESSAGE_BACKEND=kafka.

vars.name: KAFKA_SASL_ENABLED
vars.description: Enable SASL authentication for Kafka connections. Required for most managed Kafka/Redpanda services.

vars.name: KAFKA_SASL_MECHANISM
vars.description: SASL mechanism: scram-sha-256 or scram-sha-512. Required when KAFKA_SASL_ENABLED=true.

vars.name: KAFKA_SASL_USERNAME
vars.description: SASL username for Kafka authentication. Required when KAFKA_SASL_ENABLED=true.

vars.name: KAFKA_SASL_PASSWORD
vars.description: SASL password for Kafka authentication. Required when KAFKA_SASL_ENABLED=true.

vars.name: KAFKA_TLS_ENABLED
vars.description: Enable TLS encryption for Kafka connections. Required for most managed Kafka/Redpanda services.

vars.name: KAFKA_TLS_INSECURE
vars.description: Skip TLS certificate verification. For development only — never use in production.

vars.name: KAFKA_TLS_CA_PATH
vars.description: Path to CA certificate file for verifying the Kafka broker's TLS certificate.

vars.name: DATABASE_URL
vars.description: PostgreSQL connection URL (postgres://user:pass@host:5432/db). Required for services that use the database (provisioning, push).

vars.name: TESTER_PORT
vars.description: HTTP port the tester service listens on for test run management and SSE metric streaming.

vars.name: TESTER_AUTH_TOKEN
vars.description: no default — must be explicitly set for production

vars.name: GATEWAY_URL
vars.description: WebSocket URL of the gateway that test connections will target.

vars.name: PROVISIONING_URL
vars.description: HTTP base URL of the provisioning service used to create test tenants and API keys.

vars.name: TESTER_SIGNING_KEY_FILE
vars.description: Path to Ed25519 private key file for signing license tokens in the license-reload test suite. Leave empty when not running license tests.

vars.name: TESTER_ADMIN_KEY_FILE
vars.description: Path to Ed25519 private key file for admin JWT signing. When unset, an ephemeral keypair is generated per test run (local dev only).

vars.name: TESTER_ADMIN_KEY_ID
vars.description: Key ID embedded in admin JWTs; must match the kid registered with the provisioning service.

vars.name: TESTER_JWT_LIFETIME
vars.description: Lifetime of JWTs issued by the tester. Must be positive and greater than TESTER_JWT_REFRESH_BEFORE.

vars.name: TESTER_JWT_REFRESH_BEFORE
vars.description: How early before JWT expiry the tester proactively refreshes tokens to avoid auth gaps during long-running tests.

vars.name: TESTER_KEY_EXPIRY
vars.description: Lifetime of API keys created by the tester for test runs. Must be >= TESTER_JWT_LIFETIME.

vars.name: TESTER_AUTH_MODE
vars.description: Credential mode for WebSocket connections: jwt, api-key, upgrade (JWT → API key), or mixed (ratio-split).

vars.name: TESTER_API_KEY
vars.description: Pre-provisioned static API key for api-key, upgrade, and mixed auth modes. Required when TESTER_AUTH_MODE is api-key or upgrade.

vars.name: TESTER_AUTH_MIX_RATIO
vars.description: Fraction of connections using JWT in mixed mode (0.0 = all API key, 1.0 = all JWT).

vars.name: TESTER_AUTH_UPGRADE_TIMEOUT
vars.description: Timeout for the JWT-to-API-key upgrade handshake in upgrade mode.

vars.name: TESTER_GATEWAY_METRICS_URL
vars.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint of the gateway pod. When empty, metric-drift checks in revocation suites are skipped (recorded as skipped_checks, not failures).

vars.name: TESTER_GATEWAY_METRICS_INTERVAL
vars.description: Interval for polling gateway Prometheus metrics during revocation load tests.
